reviews.review_description: I actually bought this but never played it, got Drakkenridge and loved it so went back and played this and while its much shorter than Drakkenridge and I miss some of the abilities in that game if you liked Drakkenridge you'll like this. And if you like this, go check out Drakkenridge its by the same Dev and even bigger and better!

I can't believe how low this game is rated. It plays like a old rpg game. It's not a super long experience but man is it fun. I keep finding myself playing it for the arena mode now that I've beaten it. If you're thinking about buying it, do it.

reviews.review_title: Decent little RPG with good gameplay
reviews.review_description: I played for the first time for an hour this evening.  Initially, I was underwhelmed, particularly by the 2d pixelated NPCs and monsters, but something about the atmosphere, fighting mechanics, and spells, kept me playing a bit longer.  The first quest, killing some rats in a cellar, was quite cool, and made me realise that both the game mechanics, sound, and music, actually made for a moderately challenging and enjoyable experience.  The first dungeon had a degree of challenge, although I did find that there were too many health and mana chests.  I was about to put it away for now, when I got the first main dungeon quest, beneath an old church, and having completed that, I have to say that I really enjoyed it, and also have a much better idea about what the game is like.  The atmosphere, while somewhat over the top, is actually excellently done, and the sounds, perhaps a bit cliched, and also over the top, are also cleverly designed and work really well together.  The enemies somehow manage to be sufficiently convincing, despite being flat 2d pixels that advance towards you, and explode into 'gibs' and bones in a way that is quite satisfying, although perhaps that could have been made a little more gory.  And the game is reasonably fast paced which helps get you a bit into the 'zone', almost like an old 'boomer shooter' does, although at this stage it certainly is not that.  It's fun, and the $10 price tag is probably fair.  I got access via the meta quest + subscription, and this is one I will almost certainly pick up on sale.
Game is poopy an bugly
It's was ok but I would like my money back

This game does two things right. It eases you in by following the KISS principle. It also lets you cast magic with a regenerating magic bar so that you are encouraged to use magic but are limited by the slow regeneration so as not to over power you. 
If the 8-bit NPC’s don’t bother you then this is a great game to play.

If you like the retro style, then it is a lot of fun.
It was released before many of the current larger fantasy titles, but still manages to deliver a good time if you enjoy the pixel art like I do.
The style works surprisingly well in VR.

reviews.review_title: Visually awesome but a bit lacking
reviews.review_description:
This is the kind of game that if you got it on the PC in 2002 (obvs not in VR) you'd think it was amazing. However it's very shallow and the enemies and very repetitive and a tad dull to fight. Maybe I should have given it more time but the first hour was very limited. 

Love the art style and works really well in VR but the game is very lacking in the world building itself. Almost feels are your playing a tech demo than a full game.
The game is extremely laggy. Loved the environment for the first few minutes but there’s too much movement lag which made me delete the game within 10 mins of play. Hopefully this will be fixed.
Blocky I like good graphics  unlike this
I absolutely love this game, from talking to NPCs to battling creatures and completing quests. This is a definite recommend. Only thing I wish it had was Achievements!!

developer_response.body: Thanks a LOT for your review :)

reviews.review_description:
the combat system is awful:
1. you cannot have the shield on one hand and casting spell on the other hand at the same time.
2. you have to switch to spell mode to interact with objects.
3. pressing the joystick to switch between sword and spell mode is not very ergonomic
4. the sword attack doesn't feel good. 
5. enemies like to jump on your back. So if you have a small room it is very annoying and uncomfortable to play against them. 
6. it won't recognize your drawing while you moving
This game surprised me I don't like the mine craft style graphics  but the game is good and I like it good adventure game

Surprisingly this low cost game has better mechanics then a few triple A titles in the VR  space. Lack of smooth turning option is not so good but at least the snap turn is quick and walking has a smooth locomotion option. The graphics are a nice throwback pixel style for most in game models others have a drawn look but overall it’s esthetic is pleasing and easily draws you into the game. Story is pretty b~side and the voice acting sounds like someone had them speak into a tin can outside of that the effects are decent and environmental effects are good at setting the stage. It’s amazingly fun for the $6 I paid and worth the money.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the nice review! :) Smooth turning is coming soon!
Feel free to join us on our server: https://discord.gg/JQgYrW8VRd

reviews.review_description: Not gonna write a long review but all i have to say is i love this game it’s extremely addicting and i really enjoy it for everything it is, the graphics give me a nice classic graphics feel to the game which is pleasant to look at, the combat is pretty fun, and the story is over all amazing, i would recommend the game to anyone!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the nice review! Glad you enjoyed the game :)

reviews.review_description:
I played for awhile and it's super fun. Although the simple fire spell seems hard to cast if not a perfect triangle. Also the people being in 8 bit really gave me a headache. And why is the store guy always seem like it's flipping you off?? Lol. I will finish it and have fun doing it.

Edit-- ok Dev's I get it.. but he still looks like it and I yelled at him to stop a couple times. 🤣🤣 Best response ever.

developer_response.body:
He is not flipping you off!!! :) he is raising his index to "emphasize" what he is saying ;)
We are going to have less pixelated sprites in the next update, in a month or so!

reviews.review_description:
I’ll start with the pros:
The magic system and magic fighting is perfect. I love casting spells and using their different abilities to attack and defend. I love the casting system and everything about it. It’s a challenge to cast in a fight so you can’t just spam it but it feels real and logical like you’d expect. So much better than having to search a menu for spells. Just learn the casting shape and bam! Never change this feature. 
The worlds visual style and particle effects are great. I also enjoy the sound design for the world and creatures. There’s a lot to like about this game and I hope the makers adjust and add to this game. I have a feeling it can be really good with some polish. 

Cons:
The voice acting is... not the best. 
The game design such as the turning control is lacking. 
The graphics of the sprite characters need to be slightly higher resolution due to VR and they need to have 360 degree rotation meaning you make sprites that face you and then a 45 degree view left and right. A 90 degree view left and right and so on. Basically copy old school DOOM. 
The AI needs fixing. Enemies seem dumb and just stab themselves on my sword. They should at least try to take cover and then work to swarm me. 
NPC’s need to at least acknowledge you. The worst part of the village is that no one speaks or moves. It feels dead in there. 

I like this game it just needs a little fixing here and there and a bit more polish. For the price you can’t really go wrong. It’s practically free.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the nice words. We will add some Quality of Life updates soon, and we are planning some additional content!

reviews.review_description: This is one of the games where u come home from school exited to plaaaaay it's so much fun and for 7 POUNDS!!!! Bargine this is a must bye.ut is a gem hidden in the oculus quest store🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🩸🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠🏠

developer_response.body: Thanks for the nice words, and glad we are giving you something to look forward to when coming home! :D

reviews.review_description: The game has charm and old school graphics.  I love the fact you can draw runes to load up spells really immersive and fun even if it can be a bit janky sometimes.   As much as I'm having fun for 8 quid I can't deny there are bugs in the game.  I've died 4 times now in the temple at the top left of the map as I've fallen through the floor on the edge of the "ground" into water and drowned it's been tedious.  I'm paranoid now about going near the edge...would be nice if that was fixed but it's unlikely going to happen.

reviews.review_title: Could be fun but so many issues
reviews.review_description: I want to love this game but making things work easily has become so annoying I’m regretting my purchase.  Purchased in a combo with The Wizards:  Dark Times.  The spells seem to work fairly easily, but something as simple as opening doors has become impossible.  I’m trying to do the next step, going in the mines, and the door to go in will not open.  I’ve had problems getting through the doors on all steps, so this isn’t a new issue, and I did eventually get through, but now nothing will open this door any longer.  I’ve gotten in there randomly before, but now nothing.  I give up.

developer_response.body: Hello! It may be because you are trying to "physically" open the doors? Our doors work like the other interactables in the game: you need to direct your pointer at them (not too close) until it becomes a pointer as a hand with one finger raised (and a little +) - then you press your button A on your controller. I've just checked in game, could open all doors without issue. Let us know if you still have issues?
